TSLA: HCC announces new partnership with Tesla to 'strengthen Houston's workforce'. Houston Community College (HCC) has announced a new partnership with Tesla, Inc. to train Houston's workforce for advanced manufacturing careers. The program's pilot class recently graduated, and officials plan to increase enrollment to 250 students this year. The collaboration aims to provide students with technical skills and real-world experience, with graduates from the first class working at Tesla's Megafactory Texas.

Tesla Premiums Soared in ’25; Loss Ratios 40 Pts. Worse Than Industry: Analysis. A new S&P Global Market Intelligence analysis reveals that Tesla's insurance premiums grew by 40.7% in 2025, reaching nearly $1.4 billion, largely due to a strategic shift from managing general agents to direct underwriting. Despite this significant growth, particularly a 585% jump in California, Tesla's insurance loss ratios remained about 40 points worse than the industry average for the third consecutive year. The report also highlights regulatory scrutiny in California and geographic expansion into new states.

Tesla Rival Waymo Recalls Nearly 3800 Robotaxis Over Software Issue Affecting Vehicles With Latest Self-Driving Stack. Alphabet Inc.'s Waymo is recalling 3,791 autonomous vehicles due to a software glitch that could lead robotaxis to drive onto flooded roads. The issue was identified after an incident in San Antonio where an unoccupied Waymo vehicle entered a flooded lane. Waymo is implementing software safeguards and refining extreme weather operations to prevent future occurrences, while competitors like Uber and Tesla also expand their robotaxi initiatives.
TGT: Target, Walmart, More Renovate Stores to Win Back Customers. Major retailers like Target, Walmart, and Dollar General are investing heavily in store renovations to attract more customers. These upgrades include improved displays, wider aisles, better lighting, and more efficient layouts to support increased online order pickups. The initiatives aim to enhance the in-store shopping experience and convenience.

Oakland loses its only Gap store. Oakland's last remaining Gap store on Lakeshore Avenue is scheduled to close this summer after 26 years. This closure follows other major retailers like Target, Sweet July, and Lululemon leaving Oakland, with some citing safety and theft concerns. The Gap spokesperson confirmed the closure but did not provide a reason.

Target faces tougher sales backdrop after tax refund boost fades. Target Corp (NYSE:TGT) is expected to report strong first-quarter results, but Bank of America forecasts a more challenging sales environment in the second quarter as the impact of tax refunds diminishes. The bank maintained its "Underperform" rating, raising its price objective to $110 and increasing its Q1 earnings per share forecast. Analysts are cautious about decelerating sales trends, lingering effects of high gas prices, and the lapping of pricing tailwinds from tariffs in the latter half of 2026.
SBUX: Vornado JV Refinance Meatpacking Offices for $161M. Vornado Realty Trust's joint venture has secured a new $161-million refinancing for 61 Ninth Ave. in the Meatpacking District, a property fully leased to Aetna and Starbucks. This interest-only loan replaces a previous $155-million loan and carries a variable interest rate tied to SOFR. The refinancing follows a recent extension and partial paydown of a $167.5-million loan secured in 2019.

Starbucks Corp stock (US8552441094): Shares up 26.6% YTD amid union news and debt tender. Starbucks Corp shares have climbed 26.6% year-to-date to $106.58, driven by US labor developments, expansion into Chile, and a significant $1.1 billion debt tender offer. The company's performance reflects its resilience amidst rising labor costs and unionization pressures, alongside continued global demand for premium coffee. Investors are monitoring upcoming earnings and further labor developments to gauge future insights.
DIS: Phunware names new CEO, taps Disney+ veteran for AI platform push. Phunware (NASDAQ:PHUN) has appointed Dmitry Kroshka as its new CEO and engaged an AI-first product consultancy led by a former Disney+ executive, Michael Cerda, to advance its mobile guest intelligence platform. Kroshka will lead the company's "2.0 Strategy," focusing on transforming its mobile and customer engagement capabilities into an AI-enabled platform for large property operators in hospitality and healthcare. This move aims to sharpen Phunware's commercial focus on "Guest Intelligence" and build upon its AI Concierge module, which has seen strong engagement since its January release.

Bank Julius Baer & Co. Ltd Zurich Lowers Stock Holdings in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Bank Julius Baer & Co. Ltd Zurich decreased its holdings in The Walt Disney Company by 3.1% in the fourth quarter, selling 46,681 shares, though other institutional investors boosted their positions. Despite this, institutional investors still hold 65.71% of Disney's shares. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a target price of $134.47, following Disney's recent beat on quarterly earnings and revenue estimates.

The Walt Disney Company $DIS Shares Sold by Atria Investments Inc. Atria Investments Inc. decreased its holdings in The Walt Disney Company ($DIS) by 14.6% in the fourth quarter, selling 27,535 shares. Despite this, Disney's recent earnings exceeded expectations, reporting an EPS of $1.57 and revenue of $25.17 billion, while analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $134.47. The stock is currently trading at $104.90, down 1.2%.

Allworth Financial LP Has $38.35 Million Holdings in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Allworth Financial LP significantly increased its stake in The Walt Disney Company, now holding over $38 million worth of shares. This move aligns with broader institutional interest, as major investors like Vanguard and State Street also boosted their positions, collectively owning 65.71% of Disney's stock. The article highlights positive sentiment driven by analyst upgrades, strong earnings, and progress in streaming profitability, alongside some neutral and negative news regarding organizational changes and FCC scrutiny.

Walt Disney Co. stock underperforms Wednesday when compared to competitors. Shares of Walt Disney Co. (DIS) fell 1.19% on Wednesday, closing at $104.90, while the S&P 500 Index rose and the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell. The stock is currently 15.87% below its 52-week high of $124.69, reached on June 30th. This performance indicates an underperformance compared to the broader market trends on that day.
